operational_commit_kinds.label: auto-checkpoint
operational_commit_kinds.description: A worker-local checkpoint commit created by the team runtime to preserve dirty worktree changes.

operational_commit_kinds.label: integration merge
operational_commit_kinds.description: A leader-side runtime merge commit that integrates a worker branch or checkpoint into the team branch.

operational_commit_kinds.label: integration cherry-pick
operational_commit_kinds.description: A leader-side runtime cherry-pick used when the normal worker merge path cannot be used cleanly.

operational_commit_kinds.label: cross-rebase
operational_commit_kinds.description: A runtime rebase operation that moves worker work across the current leader branch baseline.

operational_commit_kinds.label: worker clean rebase
operational_commit_kinds.description: A runtime rebase that refreshes a clean worker branch onto the current leader branch baseline.

operational_commit_kinds.label: leader integration attempt
operational_commit_kinds.description: A leader-side integration attempt recorded for auditability even when it does not create a final semantic commit.

operational_commit_kinds.label: shutdown checkpoint
operational_commit_kinds.description: A shutdown-time checkpoint commit that preserves remaining worker worktree changes before cleanup.

operational_commit_kinds.label: shutdown merge
operational_commit_kinds.description: A shutdown-time runtime merge that preserves worker changes on the leader branch before teardown.

operational_commit_statuses.label: applied
operational_commit_statuses.description: The runtime operation changed repository history or preserved worker changes as intended.

operational_commit_statuses.label: no-op
operational_commit_statuses.description: The runtime operation was unnecessary because there was no relevant change to preserve or integrate.

operational_commit_statuses.label: conflict
operational_commit_statuses.description: The runtime operation encountered conflicts that require human or leader-side reconciliation.

operational_commit_statuses.label: skipped
operational_commit_statuses.description: The runtime intentionally skipped the operation because prerequisites or safety checks were not met.

task_summary.description: read-only support only. Leader is implementing in main workspace.

operational_entries.detail: source already reachable from leader HEAD
source already reachable from leader HEAD

recommended_next_steps: Inspect the current branch diff/log and identify which runtime-originated commits should be squashed or rewritten.
Derive semantic commit boundaries from completed task subjects, code diffs, and shutdown reports rather than from omx(team) operational commit subjects.
Create final commit messages in Lore format with intent-first subjects and only the trailers that add decision context.
leader_finalization_prompt: Team "ai-counselor-technical-phase1" is ready for commit finalization. Treat runtime-originated commits (auto-checkpoints, merge/cherry-picks, cross-rebases, worker clean rebase scaffolds, leader integration signals, shutdown checkpoints) as temporary scaffolding rather than final history. Do not reuse operational commit subjects verbatim. Use the completed task descriptions and resulting diffs to infer semantic commit boundaries. Rewrite or squash the operational history into clean Lore-format final commit(s) with intent-first subjects and relevant trailers. Use task subjects/results and shutdown diff reports to choose semantic commit boundaries and rationale.
